goal: Minimize AI cost while keeping enough capability for each OTTO task.

opencode.use_for: backend code generation, file creation, terminal commands, API routes, database files

cheap.purpose: Simple edits, summaries, routing, documentation, quick commands.

standard.purpose: Normal coding, small features, tests, refactors.
standard.codex: GPT-5 mini or GPT-5 when needed (gpt-4o-mini / gpt-4o)
standard.opencode: opencode — free backend code generation and file writing

strong.purpose: Complex backend, database, architecture, security, analytics, hard debugging.
strong.opencode: opencode — preferred free tool for backend file generation and API creation

deep.purpose: Very high-risk architecture, security review, production incident analysis.
deep.opencode: opencode with strongest available model for critical backend review

visual.purpose: Screenshots, UI inspection, OCR, image understanding, visual design.
visual.opencode: not recommended for visual tasks — use Claude or Codex for OCR/screenshots

tool.purpose: File generation, repository automation, local agent tooling.
tool.opencode: opencode — primary free tool for all file/code generation and terminal tasks

escalationRules: Start with the cheapest sufficient profile.
Escalate only when the current profile is likely to fail or has failed once.
Use deep only for high-risk or production-critical work.
Use visual only when images, screenshots, UI inspection, or OCR are required.
If the platform cannot switch models automatically, follow this file as manual routing guidance.
